issues.description: The translation uses '冷液分配单元' in Paragraph 4 but switches to '冷量分配单元' in Paragraph 14.
issues.why_it_matters: Consistency is key in technical whitepapers. '冷液分配单元' (Coolant Distribution Unit) is the more standard hardware-centric term in data center engineering, whereas '冷量' refers to cooling capacity.
issues.suggestion: Standardize on '冷液分配单元' throughout the text.

issues.description: Translated as '优化整个液压路径'.
issues.why_it_matters: In Chinese technical nomenclature, '液压' (Hydraulic) almost exclusively refers to high-pressure oil systems (e.g., machinery, brakes). For water/coolant cooling systems in data centers, '水力' (Hydraulic/Water-related) is the precise term used by HVAC and thermal engineers.
issues.suggestion: Change '液压路径' to '水力路径'.

issues.description: Translated as '2 英寸的快速接头'.
issues.why_it_matters: While correct, in professional hardware contexts, it is common to keep the unit with the number or use the technical symbol (") for a more 'hardcore' look.
issues.suggestion: Consider '2 英寸 (2") 快速接头' or simply '2 英寸快速接头' without the '的'.
